rxn_class_summary: Electrophotocatalytic and electrochemical cascade selenylation/cyclization of 3-aza-1,5-dienes with diaryl diselenides to give 3-selenomethyl-4-pyrrolin-2-ones under three protocols (4CzIPN photoelectrocatalysis, PC-free direct photolysis, or NHPI-mediated electrolysis).
electrochemical_role: Method A: anode oxidizes the cyclized C-radical II to cation III (with EnT from 4CzIPN* generating PhSe• from (PhSe)2); Method B: anode oxidizes radical II while 395 nm light homolyzes (PhSe)2 directly; Method C: anode oxidizes NHPI to NHPI•+ which oxidizes (PhSe)2 to PhSe• and PhSe+; cathode reduces H+ to H2 in all cases.

key_intermediates.name: β-selenyl carbon radical I (from PhSe• addition to acrylamide alkene)
Cyclized α-amido C-centered radical II (pyrrolinone radical)

electron_flow: In method A, 4CzIPN* transfers triplet energy to (PhSe)2 giving 2a* which homolyzes to PhSe•; PhSe• adds to the acrylamide alkene of 1a, the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the enamide to give radical II, which is oxidized to cation III by 4CzIPN•+/anode/NHPI•+ depending on method, followed by deprotonation to product and cathodic H+→H2. A parallel ionic pathway proceeds via PhSe+ + alkene → seleniranium IV → cation III.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction not fully suppressed (yields 60/40/37% under A/B/C), indicating radical pathway is operative but not exclusive.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a completely suppressed under all three conditions.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-Diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Partial inhibition; phenylselanyl-DPE adduct 10 isolated in 9–15%, confirming PhSe• generation.

probe_reagents.name: 4-Iodoanisole
probe_reagents.observation: PhSe-anisole adduct detected by GC-MS upon 395 nm irradiation of 2a, confirming PhSe• under PC-free photolysis.

probe_reagents.name: Azulene
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; suppresses reaction under A and B, supporting EnT/triplet involvement.

probe_reagents.name: Ferrocene (Fc)
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; trace product under A and B.

probe_reagents.name: Biisobutenyl
probe_reagents.observation: Triplet quencher; trace under A, 31% under B.

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl
probe_reagents.observation: Reacts with 1a in absence of light/electricity to give 3a in 88%, supporting PhSe+ / seleniranium ionic pathway.

probe_reagents.name: PhSeH
probe_reagents.observation: Affords 3a under A/B/C in 24/60/8% over 9 h, supporting selenolate/radical pathways.

probe_reagents.name: Diselenide scrambling ((PhSe)2 + (MeSe)2)
probe_reagents.observation: Greatly accelerated under 395 nm vs dark, supporting photohomolysis of Se–Se bond.

notes: Three mechanisms proposed in parallel for methods A/B/C. Favored pathway under A is triplet–triplet energy transfer from 4CzIPN* to (PhSe)2 rather than SET (SET from 2a to 4CzIPN* is thermodynamically unfavorable: E_red*(4CzIPN*) = 1.51 V vs Ep/2(2a) = 1.52 V). DFT triplet energies (4CzIPN 46.1, 2a 32.7 kcal/mol) support exergonic EnT. Both radical (PhSe• addition/cyclization/oxidation) and ionic (PhSe+/seleniranium) pathways may operate concurrently, as TEMPO does not fully suppress reaction and PhSeCl directly gives product. NHPI in method C functions as anodic electron shuttle (not cathodic) based on CV. Cathodic H2 evolution detected by gas detector closes the redox balance.
